zero-alloc.reason: Any heap allocation (new, LINQ, boxing) risks GC stalls in per-frame code.

unmanaged-structs.rule: Unmanaged structs only in unsafe contexts
unmanaged-structs.reason: This file uses unmanaged memory (pointers, NativeMemory). Types must satisfy the unmanaged constraint.

thread-safe.reason: Platform/driver layer may be called from multiple threads. Shared state must be synchronized.

managed-collections-in-hot-path.bad: Dictionary<K,V>, List<T>, or any managed collection
managed-collections-in-hot-path.good: Flat array with const capacity + index-based access, or pre-allocated pools
managed-collections-in-hot-path.why: Managed collections allocate on the heap and trigger GC in per-frame code paths.

linq-in-hot-path.why: Every LINQ call allocates an iterator and closure on the heap.

fixed-capacity-pool.name: Fixed-capacity pool
fixed-capacity-pool.description: Pre-allocated flat array with const Max capacity. Silent overflow (drop, don't throw) when full.

sidecar-buffer-offset-count.name: Sidecar buffer (offset+count)
sidecar-buffer-offset-count.description: Related data stored in parallel flat arrays, referenced by offset and count fields in the parent struct.

bit-packed-sort-key.name: Bit-packed sort key
bit-packed-sort-key.description: Multiple sort dimensions packed into a single integer using bit shifts for zero-allocation sorting.

unsafe-memory.name: Unsafe memory
unsafe-memory.description: Direct pointer manipulation for performance-critical code paths.

native-memory-allocation.name: Native memory allocation
native-memory-allocation.description: NativeMemory.Alloc/Free for unmanaged heap allocations outside GC control.

fixed-buffer-in-struct.name: Fixed buffer in struct
fixed-buffer-in-struct.description: Fixed-size buffer embedded in struct for inline storage without heap allocation.
